creator: karl@pielorz.com
text:
Hi,

I just went to try this. To make sure the issue was still there - I installed a 10.3-RELEASE system, installed 'xe-guest-utilities' etc. (Xen shows it as an Agile VM now).

I setup some test code to display the local clock + a remote machines clock - and live migrated the 10.3 machine. It crashed when it landed:

panic: reserved (unknown fault)
... kbd_backtrace
... vpanic
... trap_fatal
... trap
... calltrap
... xctrl_suspend
... xctrl_on_watch_event
... xenwatch_thread
... fork_exit
... fork_tampoline


This was before I applied the patches - we don't have any 10.3 boxes running under Xen - so we'd not come across the issue before. The plan was to verify the time issue is still there in 10.3 - then take that box to 10-STABLE, verify it again - apply the patches - and see what happens.

But I guess I'll have to file a bug report for 10.3 now with all the details for the panic.

To prove my sanity I live migrated a 10.1-RELEASE-p10 box on the same pool - works fine. A 10.2-RELEASE-p14 VM also migrates fine.

I'll install another 10.3-R VM from scratch - but at this point, I'm unable to test the patch.

-Karl
